title: Reusable Client Architecture, Transport Abstractions, Middleware, and Configuration · Browser and Node Client Systems · Retrieval Circuit
summary: A design-system maintainer reconstructing an offline failure handoff must reconstruct the governing policy from partial evidence after delay and distinguish current rules from tempting earlier ones for reusable client architecture, transport abstractions, middleware, and configuration, producing small composable client library boundary. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch small composable client library boundary after a delayed changed case. Lea…
objectives: Centralize base authority, transport, timeout, user agent, credentials, limits, and defaults in an explicit client configuration. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Inject the narrow request execution capability needed for deterministic tests without cloning the whole HTTP library. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Compose authentication, retries, metrics, tracing, caching, and redaction with documented ordering and ownership. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Fetch and TypeScript Client Configuration · changed case · Review Client Configuration

review case — offline design-system client library needs base authority, injected transport, auth, retry, cache, metrics, redaction, and typed operation T-2.
